utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . maria martin , date of birth 03/05/1946 , has been informed and consents to have this visit recorded . the patient is here for follow-up .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , maria . how have you been doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: same i guess . pretty good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so you got my letter last time on the results . your sugars are not very well controlled .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i got them .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so did you increase your glimepiride ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: did i increase it ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm still taking the two milligrams .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . so we want you to , as the letter said , from the last time , when your a1c was 9.9 , we wanted you to increase it to four milligrams twice a day , so let's start that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sure . so you want me to take two of the pills , now twice a day ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes . uh , how much of it do you have ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i just filled it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . so yeah , you can take two of them twice a day , of what you have . the new prescription will be for four milligrams twice a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . and are you trying to eat healthier : less sugars , less carbohydrates ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , well ...

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: not really ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: . well , i have n't had any pasta ; i just do n't really like it , so we do n't have any in the house . we do n't often have bread , and if we do , we buy wheat bread .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . but even the wheat bread , try to cut down on the number of slices you eat per day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i just usually have two in the morning . i do n't care for sandwiches much ; maybe peanut butter sandwiches once in a while .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i hardly drink , drink any sodas .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , that's great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i drink club soda .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: seltzer water , that's what i prefer , so that's my water . i do n't drink regular water .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , that's fine . how about your eye exams ? are they up-to-date ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep . i just got new glasses .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . who do you go see ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i go to liberty road , the group up there .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . the , um , the optimum optometrists , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they keep changing doctors each time i go .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: a new doc each time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i got a really nice one i used to see a year ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: was that dr. sanchez ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you saw him not too long ago ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i just got my prescription from him . well , see , i had the iodine for my eyes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: cataracts , yep , okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so he did that and then i had to wait for a month to get healed up and then he wrote my prescription .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: perfect . and then you've got the high blood pressure .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . a couple of weeks ago , it was 116 on the dot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , we will recheck it . you're on a combination of medications . you are on the met- met- uh , metoprolol , 50 milligrams once a day , you're on the lisinopril with hydrochlorothiazide , at 20 , with 25 milligrams once a day , and you're on the amlodipine , 10 milligram once a day . are you taking all of those ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: perfect . and then you've got the high cholesterol and the vascular disease in your legs . you're on atorvastatin . are you tolerating it okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , but honestly , my feet feel like hell .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all the time , my legs are bugging me ; i ca n't do much walking .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . yeah , the feet i think is what we call peripheral neuropathy .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: also , i have the pain up to my hip .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . you have pain in the hips and upper legs ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , all of it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so those do seem relatively stable , right ? and are you trying to get the walking in ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you are smoking still ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , still smoking .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: not ready to quit ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not really .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's a bad combination .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i know . i'll think about it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . now , let's do my review of systems . you're not having any headaches or dizziness ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: headaches or dizziness , no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nothing , feeling totally normal .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good , good . and the vision is better with the cataract surgery ? that's been good ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . not happy with this lens ; it's kinda funny . the other one is good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmm , sometimes they can go in and clean up the eye with laser , but we'll see what they say .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . no shortness of breath or coughing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been coughing here lately because i've been congested .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what does it look like when it comes up ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's relatively clear .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no fever , chills , no shortness of breath ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . no chest pains or pressure or tightness ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: nausea , vomiting , gi symptoms ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . peeing okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not good . it's , you know , i'm old ; it takes me a little more time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: slower stream but stable ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , but i do pee ...

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: . several times per day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm . how many times do you have to get up at night ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not too bad , just once in a while .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: then i have to pee by morning . i do tend to drink my drinks in the evening .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , let's have a look at you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: physical exam : carotids are normal , pulse is no buits . heart exam : rhythm is regular , normal rate , s1 and s2 are crisp . i do not hear any murmurs today , okay . have you lean forward for me . take some deep breaths , in and out . okay , lungs are clear . i do n't hear any rasps or wheezing today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my coughing got better this morning .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you cleared out the lungs ? all right , let me take a look at your feet .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . extremities are without edema . pulses are palpable on the dorsalis pedis posterial tibial . she has thickened nails .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . mildly dry skin , no skin breakdown . l- let me know if you can feel this .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i closed my eyes . yeah , yeah , okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mildly decreased sensation in the tips of the first and second toes . okay , we're done with your exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so again , try to keep the feet moisturized .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you do have a little bit of periphe- peripheral neuropathy . um , there is a medication we can use if they get really bad , but you're already on so many medications .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my girl that i talk to and stuff , she uses on her feet ; she really likes it . it's , like , a foot scrub or some type of cream . i put it on when i think of it after the shower . so i could get my own instead .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that sounds good . so for now , your sugars , we're gon na check your ... we're going to recheck your blood work today , but the sugars last time we checked , the a1c , your blood sugar average was above nine . i really want you to increase the glimepiride . so the ones you have at home , the two milligram , take two of them twice a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . watch for low sugars , weak , shaky , sweaty . if that happens , let us know , and i do n't expect it to .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . the new prescription that i'm going to write is gon na be for the , uh , four milligram pill . so when you get the new one , just one pill twice a day . also , continue the metformin , okay ? blood pressure , when i rechecked it , i got 140 over 60 s- so the numbers at home are better , so we're gon na continue the combination of medications . we'll check kidney function today .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your cholesterol though , you're on the high dose of atorvastatin or lipitor to protect the arteries , so we really wan na keep you on that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , no problem .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i want you to quit smoking , but we've had this battle before .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmm . and then , we will leave everything else the same . we'll get the labs today and we'll see you back here in four months . sound good ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . have a good day , ma'am . take care of yourself .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you . you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: bye .

detail_list.summary: Maria Martin is a 75-year-old female presenting for a follow-up visit.
Diabetes management.
The patient's blood glucose is poorly controlled with a recent A1C of 9.9%.
The doctor asks if the patient increased her glimepiride dosage.
The patient confirms she is still taking 2 mg of glimepiride.
The doctor instructs the patient to increase glimepiride to 4 mg twice a day.
The patient is instructed to take two 2 mg glimepiride tablets twice daily, and a new prescription for 4 mg tablets will be provided.
The doctor asks if the patient is reducing sugar and carbohydrate intake.
The patient reports she does not eat pasta, rarely eats bread, and prefers wheat bread when she does.
The patient reports she drinks club soda and seltzer water instead of soda.
The doctor asks if the patient's eye exams are up to date.
The patient confirms her eye exams are up to date and she recently received new glasses.
The patient confirms she sees Dr. Sanchez for her eye care.
The patient reports she recently underwent cataract surgery.
Hypertension.
The patient reports a home blood pressure reading of 116 mmHg a few weeks ago.
The patient is taking metoprolol 50 mg daily, lisinopril/hydrochlorothiazide 20/25 mg daily, and amlodipine 10 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient is compliant with her blood pressure medications.
Hyperlipidemia and peripheral vascular disease.
The doctor asks if the patient is tolerating atorvastatin.
The patient reports she is tolerating atorvastatin but experiences significant foot and leg pain.
The doctor identifies the patient's foot pain as peripheral neuropathy.
The patient reports pain extending to her hips.
The doctor asks if the patient is still smoking.
The patient confirms she is still smoking.
The doctor asks if the patient is ready to quit smoking.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing headaches or dizziness.
The doctor asks if the patient's vision has improved following cataract surgery.
The patient reports improvement in one eye but dissatisfaction with the lens in the other eye.
The doctor asks if the patient has shortness of breath or a cough.
The patient reports a productive cough due to recent congestion.
The doctor asks if the patient is having urinary symptoms.
The patient reports a slower urine stream which she attributes to aging.
The doctor asks how often the patient gets up at night to urinate.
The patient reports occasional nocturia, which she attributes to evening fluid intake.
Physical exam findings: normal carotids, regular heart rhythm, clear lungs, no edema, palpable pulses, and thickened nails.
Physical exam findings: mildly decreased sensation in the tips of the first and second toes bilaterally.
The doctor advises the patient to keep her feet moisturized.
The doctor orders blood work to recheck A1C and kidney function.
The doctor counsels the patient on signs of hypoglycemia.
The doctor confirms the patient will continue her current blood pressure and cholesterol medications.
The patient is scheduled for a follow-up visit in four months.
